task_completion.description: Verify that the required local, remote, and virtual repositories were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the Administration -> Repositories list or the Artifacts tree showing 'medsecure-dev', 'medsecure-staging', 'medsecure-prod', 'maven-central-proxy', and 'medsecure-maven-all'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required groups and user were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the Administration -> Identity and Access -> Groups page showing 'platform-engineers' and 'qa-team', and the Users page showing 'eng-sarah'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the permission targets were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the Administration -> Identity and Access -> Permissions page showing 'deploy-perms' and 'qa-perms' in the list.

task_completion.description: Verify that the commons-io artifact was deployed to the dev repository and copied to staging and prod.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the Artifacts tree browser showing 'commons-io-2.15.1.jar' inside 'medsecure-dev', 'medsecure-staging', and 'medsecure-prod' under the path 'org/apache/commons/commons-io/2.15.1/'. You should see the user uploading the file and copying the artifact between repositories using the UI.

task_completion.description: Verify that the SMTP mail server was configured with the correct settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot of the Administration -> General -> Mail Server page with Host: 'smtp.medsecure.example.com', Port: '587', TLS enabled, From: 'artifactory@medsecure.example.com', and Subject Prefix: '[MedSecure-Artifacts]'.

task_completion.description: Verify that anonymous access was disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot of the Administration -> Security -> Settings page where the 'Allow Anonymous Access' checkbox is unchecked and saved.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Artifactory Web UI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be navigating the Artifactory web interface in a browser, not using curl or scripts in a terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent uploaded the actual artifact file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should select the 'commons-io-2.15.1.jar' file from the Desktop during the upload process, rather than creating a fake file or skipping the upload.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (F12) should not be open to modify the DOM and fake the presence of repositories, users, or artifacts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires setting up a JFrog Artifactory environment via its Web UI at http://localhost:8082. Repositories to create: Local ('medsecure-dev', 'medsecure-staging', 'medsecure-prod'), Remote ('maven-central-proxy'), Virtual ('medsecure-maven-all'). Groups: 'platform-engineers', 'qa-team'. User: 'eng-sarah' (in 'platform-engineers'). Permissions: 'deploy-perms' (Deploy, Read, Delete for platform-engineers on dev/staging), 'qa-perms' (Read for qa-team on staging/prod). Artifact: 'commons-io-2.15.1.jar' must be uploaded to 'medsecure-dev' at 'org/apache/commons/commons-io/2.15.1/' and copied to 'medsecure-staging' and 'medsecure-prod'. SMTP: host 'smtp.medsecure.example.com', port '587', TLS enabled, from 'artifactory@medsecure.example.com', prefix '[MedSecure-Artifacts]'. Security: Anonymous access must be disabled.
